# Claude from Codex

> A plain Codex skill for using local Claude Code as a second opinion, code reviewer, or parallel coding agent from inside Codex.

Claude from Codex lets Codex call the user's local Claude Code CLI for read-only review, plan feedback, failure investigation, documentation review, and bounded implementation passes. It works as a normal skill folder and does not require a Codex plugin login.

## Start Here

- [README](README.md): install instructions, quick start, basic usage, and troubleshooting.
- [Skill instructions](skills/claude-from-codex/SKILL.md): the actual `claude-from-codex` skill that Codex loads.
- [Copy-paste examples](docs/examples.md): prompts for installing the skill, reviewing a diff, getting a second opinion, investigating tests, and running background work.
- [Optional companion CLI](docs/companion-cli.md): advanced diagnostics, environment filtering, and background job commands.

## Installation

Ask Codex to install:

```text
Install the Codex skill from https://github.com/yujingz/use-claude-code-from-codex.
Prefer the vercel-labs/skills installer if it is available.
Install the skill named claude-from-codex for Codex, then verify that $claude-from-codex is discoverable.
```

Terminal install:

```sh
npx skills add yujingz/use-claude-code-from-codex --skill claude-from-codex -g -a codex -y
```

## Common User Requests

- "Use Claude Code from Codex."
- "Get Claude's second opinion on this plan."
- "Ask Claude to review my current diff."
- "Have Claude investigate this failing test in parallel."
- "Use Claude from Codex without installing a Codex plugin."

## Notes For Agents

- Prefer read-only Claude delegation unless the user explicitly asks for write-capable work.
- Pass a self-contained prompt to Claude: goal, relevant files, constraints, acceptance criteria, and expected return format.
- Treat Claude's answer as another review signal, not ground truth.
- Verify important claims against the workspace before acting.
- Keep Claude setup outside this repo; this skill diagnoses visibility but does not manage the user's Claude installation.
